title: Agents = LLM + tools in a loop, not frameworks

body.concept: The most successful agent implementations don't use complex frameworks. They use simple, composable patterns built directly on LLM APIs. Anthropic distinguishes two types of agentic systems: **workflows** (LLMs orchestrated through predefined code paths) and **agents** (LLMs that dynamically direct their own processes and tool usage). Both are built from the same building block: an augmented LLM with retrieval, tools, and memory.
body.analogy: Think of it like cooking. A framework is a meal-kit subscription — convenient but rigid and hard to customize. Simple patterns are knowing basic techniques (sautéing, braising, roasting). With techniques, you can cook anything; with a meal kit, you can only cook what's in the box.
body.visual: A single box labeled 'Augmented LLM' with three arrows feeding into it: 'Retrieval', 'Tools', 'Memory'. This one box is the universal building block that all patterns below are composed from.
